title: Good vs Bad Pieces
summary: Activity matters more than raw piece count

steps.title: Not All Bishops Are Equal
steps.text: A bishop blocked by its own pawns on the same color is a 'bad bishop' — cramped and ineffective. A bishop with open diagonals is a 'good bishop' — long-range firepower. The same is true for every piece: what it does is more important than what it is.

steps.title: Improve Your Worst Piece
steps.text: A useful principle from grandmaster Makagonov: 'every move, improve your worst-placed piece.' Before attacking, make sure your slowest pieces are doing something. Quiet moves to improve piece coordination often win games.
